codes.label: experimental evidence
codes.definition: Evidence type that is the inference derived from observation of the results of an experiment in a wet laboratory or computational system. Used for claims directly grounded in measurement, intervention, or experimental data.

codes.label: similarity evidence
codes.definition: Evidence type that uses an observed similarity (e.g. sequence, structural, or stimulus similarity) to support an assertion about a feature, often by transferring annotation from one entity to a similar one.

codes.label: combinatorial evidence
codes.definition: Evidence type derived from combining multiple lines of evidence (e.g. multiple modalities, multiple subjects) into a single supported assertion.

codes.label: evidence used in manual assertion
codes.definition: Evidence type used in an assertion that was reviewed or asserted by a human curator. Applies to claims whose support is established through manual human judgment rather than purely automated inference.

codes.label: inferential evidence
codes.definition: Evidence type that infers a feature or property from other evidence, including logical inference from premises in the discussion or interpretation section. Distinct from direct experimental evidence.

codes.label: evidence used in automatic assertion
codes.definition: Evidence type used in an assertion that was made by an automated procedure without human curation. Applies to claims whose support is established through automated annotation pipelines.

codes.label: high throughput evidence
codes.definition: Evidence type derived from a high-throughput experiment (e.g. genome-wide assay, large-scale imaging study). Often paired with combinatorial or similarity evidence.

codes.label: documented statement evidence
codes.definition: Evidence type based on a statement documented in the literature without direct experimental verification within the current work. Applies to claims that cite prior work as their primary support.

codes.label: computational evidence
codes.definition: Evidence type derived from a computational analysis, simulation, model, or algorithmic procedure. Includes results from neuroimaging analyses, statistical modeling, and machine-learning classifiers.
